title: Refactor browser shell for low-spec AI browser
description:
## Problem Statement
Need highly capable AI browser that runs well on low-spec hardware and can grow into browser shell for host-machine communication. Current app is good browser prototype, but shell bridge, terminal streaming, safety policy, and low-spec runtime shaping are not yet designed as one coherent path. Need preserve seamless feel, keep monolith, and avoid framework churn unless it clearly helps.

## Solution
Keep Electron for now. It already matches current stack, host-process needs, and low-spec practicality better than a framework swap. Add a browser-shell layer that exposes two-way, allowlisted host command execution with streamed terminal output into the renderer. Keep current browser UX intact, add shell surface as a first-class tool, and tune runtime for weak hardware. Leave cloud OS expansion and paper-style tab manager for later layers.

## Commits
1. Define shell contract and policy shape with no runtime behavior change.
2. Add command normalization and allowlist checks in main process.
3. Add a streaming command runner using a terminal-style process bridge, with stdout/stderr/input/exit events.
4. Expose shell IPC through preload with a minimal API, but no UI yet.
5. Add renderer shell surface scaffold hidden by default.
6. Wire open/close/focus/clear controls and stream output into the browser shell UI.
7. Add graceful stop/restart, backpressure, and session cleanup.
8. Add low-spec runtime tuning and memory/CPU guardrails without changing core navigation flow.
9. Add tests for policy, lifecycle, and IPC contract behavior.
10. Add smoke coverage for shell open, command stream, and tab/browser coexistence.
11. Update docs for shell behavior, safety model, and intended future expansion.

## Decision Document
- Electron stays for now.
- Monolith stays; no module-splitting refactor as the primary goal.
- Shell is two-way but allowlisted by default.
- Execution is local only.
- Streaming terminal behavior is required; one-shot command output is not enough.
- Existing browser shell, vertical tabs, sidecar, and extension support stay conceptually intact.
- Paper-style tab manager stays as later work, not this refactor.
- Cloud OS expansion stays as roadmap, not this step.
- Safety policy must live near command execution, not in renderer-only code.

## Testing Decisions
- Test external behavior only.
- Unit test command normalization, allowlist decisions, and shell lifecycle state.
- Unit test renderer state updates for shell stream handling.
- Smoke test tab switching plus shell open/close plus streamed output in one session.
- Reuse existing Jest style as prior art.
- Keep tests focused on visible browser-shell behavior, not internal implementation details.

## Out of Scope
- Cloud OS implementation.
- PaperWM-style tab manager implementation.
- Framework migration to CEF.
- General unrestricted shell access with no allowlist.
- Full AI backend wiring.
- Major visual redesign.
- Large-scale module split or package restructuring.

## Further Notes
Target machine is low-spec: 2-core Celeron 1000M, 3.7 GiB RAM, heavy swap use. Electron is the pragmatic choice right now because it reduces rewrite risk and supports host bridge needs without a second framework migration.
close_reason: Implemented browser shell contract, allowlisted terminal bridge, renderer shell panel, and tests.

title: Fix tab state persistence/switching
description: Tabs lose state when switching and load behavior is flaky. Likely cause: renderer keeps local URL/title state, but main tabManager never receives navigation updates, so broadcasts can overwrite newer renderer state with stale values on switch/reload.
close_reason: Synced tab metadata from renderer to main, registered webview shortcuts, and added splash shortcut dropdown.

title: Add test infrastructure - Jest + sample tests for main process modules
preload/IPC mismatch - sidebar:state and sidecar:state channels have no handlers
Remove duplicate lifecycle import in ipc-handlers.js
tab-lifecycle.js tabId string vs webContents int ID mismatch - LOD broken
extension-loader.js broadcastUpdate missing extensions panel updates
Decide extension execution/isolation model
description: Need a decision on whether extensions should run in separate isolates/processes with shared network/session state, or stay directly in Chromium extension context. Clarify what Electron supports for session sharing, isolation, and extension APIs before implementation.
notes: Recommendation: direct (session.defaultSession). Doc in docs/extension-model.md. Git 915ac68.

title: Add shortcut dropdown on splash screen
description: Ctrl+L and other shortcuts are inconsistent when focus is inside webviews. Add a dropdown under speed dial on the new tab/splash screen showing available keyboard shortcuts, so users can discover them without relying on in-page capture.
close_reason: Synced tab metadata from renderer to main, registered webview shortcuts, and added splash shortcut dropdown.
